name: Andrus, Dexter Eli
description: This is of uncertain age, passable habits and holder of a political record. The only man in the world who was known to have received the telegram notifying him of his election as county surveyor while reading the letter that offered him the nomination. To be remembered at M. S. M. as the originator of the brilliant idea of allowing the Thermo class to work his thesis for him.

name: Bramson, Charles
description: The man in the Senior class that comes up for everything that he is asked for and does it with an open heart. Famous for talking so you can't understand him and for knowing more than the average man has a right to know. Also will be remembered as the fellow that made the green caps and gowns possible St. Pat's day. Came from Russia and says that he never will go back.

name: Brooks, Lyman Herbert
description: From the sheep ranges came Brooks and brought a strong dislike for the term "Herder." Though lovingly christened "Lyman Herbert," the handle has not been much used as he is mostly known as "Gov." For two years he was girl shy, but finally succumbed, understudying Katz in the P. G. course. Being the first man to substitute Cyaniding for Hydraulics was one of his great achievements.

name: Castillon, Tirso
description: "Tirso," El Toreador! If you don't believe it we will show you the picture. Fond of the ladies and just the best fusser we ever had. Famous for telling funny stories without realizing it. Never known to worry about work and never will.

name: Cody, Frank Wesley
description: Famous for being the most silent member of the Senior class. It has been said that he resembles "Vic Hughes" in his younger days. Has the queerest little grinning laugh and really means it. One of the few fellows that do not have any trouble in working.

name: Copelin, Leonard Stephen
description: The wizard on the violin. Is famous for having worked one of Mann's classifier problems in fifteen minutes. Is continually talking about Butte, but he makes it go. Likes to stick the Prof on some foolish question and has never been known to miss a chance of taking a holiday.

name: Clayton, Charles Yancy
description: "Boots" is the real wit of the class; if you don't believe it ask him. Besides being so distinguished, he is some gun and if you need proof apply the same test as above. The kind of fellow that you will hunt for a long time before you find his equal. Came pretty near being a steady society man, but gave it up.

name: Ehlers, William Jr.
description: Ehlers was recruited from Washington U. in his third year and sought to be a hero by taking C. E. Is at present uncertain whether he is a hero or a martyr. Has a pretty little story that he tells about how he was nicknamed. "Did" has a pitching arm of which he thinks a great deal and has done some clever work with it for the Miners' team. Famous for being the most graceful sprinter in school.

name: Gleason, Augustus Wilhelm
description: "Auggie" is famous for telling long stories and getting angry when you don't laugh. One of those unlucky fellows that do their best for what they think is right and succeed in getting results and a lot of people sore. Is noted for original ideas and especially the Rollamo "Square Dance."

name: Hopkins, James
description: Not many years ago young Jim Hopkins crawled underground and asked for a job breaking coal. Worked there until he heard of Rolla and then he immediately came to the School of Mines. Has a sort of twisted idea that knocking is all right in its place. Manages to get along all O. K., even if some of the Profs do wonder how he does it. Not much of a fusser, but says he likes them just the same.

name: Ingram, John Charavelle
description: "Jack" comes from Washington U. and has a habit of thinking that he is still there. Has all the Freshmen scared and sometimes makes his bluff work on Doc. Test. Is in for everything that counts and counts in everything he is in for. Will be remembered for the way he tickles his mandolin and for calling Metz down.

name: Katz, Howard
description: Howard. Famous for coming so near to going to school and yet never getting there. Was a gun in Mine Surveying problems, but that was a long, long time ago. Says he never had to work and don't believe in it. His specialty at present is training "Gov." in the way he should go. Has Bill Jones' job.

name: Knappenberger, William Ralph
description: In years to come, when we think of days and nights passed in Alma Mater, we will remember how the "General" used to cheer us up with such music as "Good Bye, Summer," or "When Irish Eyes are Smiling." "Knappy" has been out in the cruel world and knows what we are all up against, which is probably the reason why he wants to take some one with him this time.

name: Knickerbocker, Ray Gould
description: Who ever heard of Clymers? That's where "Knick" made his first flying tackle. Has belonged to some sort of ball team ever since he was born. In for everything that is right and the kind of man worthy to represent M. S. M.

name: Moore, Philip A.
description: One and the only one we have from that old town of "Chi" that does not show the least inclination to talk about it. A student from the very beginning, else why that magnificent brow and studious look? Has been known to cause the dear Professors some trouble by asking questions when they are least expecting them, and, all in all, is some "gun."

name: Murphy, John Andrew
description: "Murph," our Irish lad with the droll sense of humor and Irish smile. How well we will all remember his funny stories and his hearty laugh. A man who does things and does them in the right way. Originator of the G. S. Gala day and the only man in school who can render "Christopher Columbo."

name: Holman, Thompson Marshall
description: Is one of that tried and true bunch that come from the wilds of Iowa, where athletes grow on bushes and the bushes are large and numerous. Showed everybody up when it came to the broad and high jumps. Is famous for limiting himself to one letter a day and is never known to miss a chance to write every night. Will be remembered as the only fellow that ever dared smoke in Doc. Test's lab.

name: Nowlan, Harry Hackett
description: Harry is too well known for us to attempt to say how much he has meant to M. S. M. The man that put the pep into this year's athletics by his efforts in organizing the rooting and seems to naturally do the right thing at the right time.

name: Radcliffe, Donald Hewson
description: Comes from that never-to-be-forgotten village of K. C. Has been known to try to dance and some say that he can. A hard worker and believes in taking long walks on Sunday afternoon. Both of which go together.

name: Seward, J. Frederick
description: Is a coal miner from that good old State of Illinois. Has a heart so big that they say he gave it to some one else to keep for him. Never was known to be without a good story and to us all is our "good fellow." Will be remembered as toastmaster at the "dinner" after the Oklahoma game at Joplin.

name: Shaw, Harry William
description: The coal mining engineer of the class is Shaw. Already served as an expert witness and is said to have appeared in court and plead his own case, had a favorable verdict returned and is to start serving time in June. He admits that she is some fine girl. Taught Forbes all he knows about coal mining.

name: Sickly, Robert Glenn
description: This "Pride of Tau Beta Pi" took up his first studies at La Harpe, Kan., and at the early age of three days made an analysis of the local milk supply. His parents, proud of his achievement, carefully educated him to send to the School of Mines. Seems to be fitted by nature to become a dancing master. His excuse for living is having made a Tau Bet out of Hopkins.

name: Wagstaff, Richard
motto: "Well, they don't do that way at the Utah Copper."
clubs: Treasurer M. S. M. A. A.
description: Did you ever wonder where Dick got that breezy manner of his when he talks of baseball and how it is played? Well, the explanation is, he was born in K. C. Has laughed ever since he was a little boy and is always happy. Is a worker and takes great delight in tangling "Boots" up in some Metallurgy problem. If we had all done as much for M. S. M. as Dick, we could be proud with reason.

name: Webster, John Nixon
description: If you do not believe that Iowa is the State of athletes, watch Johnny hold down second in the next game or get the jump on all of them in the hundred-yard dash. Made Creston City famous by subscribing for its only daily and says it is the only place worth living in outside of St. James.

name: Thadden, C. Wilson
description: Wilson is to be remembered as the Ore Dressing shark of the class and stands high in the favor of the Profs. Is remarkably quiet and we think that there might be a reason for such, although we have never heard where she lives.

name: John, W. Greene
description: "Johnny" is the sort of a fellow we will all be wishing for when we are down and out and need some one to help the good cause along. Has been known to work when he really got interested. Famous for wearing large hats and new shoes. Says that he is not Dutch, like Ehlers, and that he has held some positions, but none permanently.
